title: Cite Hall's Basis Theorem with the EXACT basic-commutator convention of the problem

comments.author: human
comments.body: Step ($\star$) relies on Hall's Basis Theorem: the basic commutators of weight exactly $w$ form a free abelian BASIS of $\gamma_w(F)/\gamma_{w+1}(F)$. Several inequivalent 'basic commutator' conventions exist. The problem fixes a specific Hall ordering: weight-monotone total order, and for $u=[u_1,u_2]$ the constraint $v\ge u_2$ in $[u,v]$ with $u>v$. The proof must verify that the version of Hall's theorem invoked matches THIS convention (Magnus-Karrass-Solitar Thm 5.13 / M. Hall Ch. 11), to avoid definitional drift. State the theorem precisely and confirm the hypotheses (free group, finite rank, this ordering).
